babyDevelopment: The baby's digestive system is getting a workout. The fetus is starting to swallow more amniotic fluid, which provides some hydration and calories. The bone marrow is now taking over blood cell production from the liver and spleen.
bodyChanges: You might experience increased appetite as baby grows faster. Some women notice 'spider veins' on their legs due to increased blood pressure. Your gums may become more prone to bleeding (pregnancy gingivitis), so be gentle when brushing.
tips: Increase your iron intake to support baby's blood cell production
Floss regularly and use a soft-bristled toothbrush
Keep up with your moderate exercise routine

do: Graze on healthy snacks throughout the day
dont: Ignore any signs of dizzy spells
Consume excessive amounts of refined sugar

travelAdvisory.note: A comfortable stage for travel; prioritize hydration.

warningSignsToWatch: Sudden, severe pain in lower abdomen
milestoneDescription: Bone marrow is now the primary site for baby's red blood cell production.
